source_filing: Federal Reserve FOMC Implementation Note, April 29, 2026
original_text: Effective April 30, 2026, the Federal Open Market Committee directs the Desk to: Undertake open market operations as necessary to maintain the federal funds rate in a target range of 3-1/2 to 3-3/4 percent.
modified_text: Effective April 30, 2026, the FOMC directed the Desk to maintain the federal funds rate in a target range of 3-3/4 to 4 percent, holding the upper bound at 4.00 percent.
claim: Effective April 30, 2026, the federal funds target range upper bound was 4.00 percent.
citation_as_written: Federal Reserve FOMC Implementation Note, April 29, 2026
notes: The period (effective April 30, 2026) and citation are correct, but the upper bound is changed from the real 3-3/4 percent (3.75%) to a plausible-but-wrong 4 percent (4.00%). A 25 bps shift to a round adjacent value is plausible to a casual reader but directly contradicts the official figure, so the claim is unsupported.
